positions.claim: Post-training and reinforcement learning scaling will be the primary driver of AI capability gains in the near term, despite lacking an equivalent corpus to pre-training data
implicitAssumptions: Synthetic task generation or human feedback can scale more efficiently than collecting additional pre-training data
The marginal returns from scaling pre-training compute have diminished relative to post-training compute
RL compute requirements grow sub-linearly compared to the capability gains they unlock

positions.claim: Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) technology will become the critical bottleneck solution for AI networking, enabling bandwidth scaling beyond copper's physical limitations
implicitAssumptions: AI model architectures will continue requiring dense all-to-all communication patterns that exceed two-rack domains
CPO manufacturing can achieve cost parity with traditional optical transceivers within the deployment timeline of next-generation AI clusters
The bandwidth requirements for AI training will grow faster than alternative solutions (like sparsity or new architectures) can compensate

positions.claim: Datacenter power availability, not chip supply, will become the primary constraint on AI deployment by 2026, with grid capacity already exhausted in key markets
implicitAssumptions: Utility-scale power infrastructure buildout timelines (3-7 years) are fundamentally incompatible with AI deployment urgency (6-18 months)
Onsite generation economics become favorable when speed-to-market advantages are factored into revenue calculations
Regulatory frameworks will permit widespread onsite generation deployment despite environmental and permitting concerns

positions.claim: Apple's strategic partnership with TSMC, where Apple funds leading-edge node development through guaranteed volume, creates an insurmountable competitive moat for both companies
implicitAssumptions: No other single customer can match Apple's combination of volume predictability and willingness to absorb early node risk
Process node leadership compounds over time because early access enables architectural advantages that persist across generations
TSMC's capacity allocation strategy permanently disadvantages competitors who cannot fund yield learning curves

positions.claim: Memory (DRAM/HBM/NAND) supply constraints will persist and intensify because chipmakers cannot build capacity fast enough to meet AI-driven demand, reversing decades of Moore's Law cost declines
implicitAssumptions: AI workload memory intensity grows faster than memory manufacturing capacity can scale, even with maximum capital deployment
The physics of memory scaling have hit fundamental limits that cannot be overcome with incremental engineering
Demand elasticity for AI compute is low enough that customers will pay dramatically higher memory prices rather than reduce deployment

positions.claim: Anthropic will dramatically outgrow OpenAI in 2026 due to Claude Code's trajectory toward capturing 20%+ of all daily code commits
implicitAssumptions: Code generation represents a winner-take-most market where workflow integration creates durable switching costs
OpenAI's distribution advantages (ChatGPT brand, Microsoft partnership) cannot overcome Claude's technical superiority in coding tasks
Developer tool revenue scales linearly or super-linearly with commit share, rather than being commoditized by competition

positions.claim: Datacenter CPU demand will experience an unexpected resurgence in late 2025 driven by reinforcement learning and code generation workloads, reversing Intel's decline
implicitAssumptions: RL and code execution workloads have fundamentally different compute characteristics that favor CPUs over GPUs for certain phases
The ratio of inference/RL compute to training compute will shift dramatically enough to materially impact CPU demand
Intel can execute competitively on process and architecture despite recent failures, given renewed market demand

positions.claim: Nvidia must pursue extreme rack-scale co-design to maintain competitive advantage as rivals catch up on system-level integration
implicitAssumptions: System-level optimization provides larger performance gains than chip-level improvements at current technology nodes
Nvidia's competitors are closing the gap on chip performance faster than Nvidia can extend leads through silicon alone
Customers will accept Nvidia's increased control over system design in exchange for performance benefits

positions.claim: Hardware infrastructure characteristics (chip microarchitecture, system architecture) fundamentally determine the economics and scalability of AI software businesses
implicitAssumptions: Software optimization cannot overcome hardware inefficiencies beyond a constant factor
AI software gross margins are more sensitive to infrastructure costs than to traditional software factors like sales efficiency or customer acquisition
Hardware architecture choices made today will lock in cost structures for multi-year periods due to software stack dependencies

positions.claim: Chipmaking innovation is being held back by organizational inertia and fear-driven decision-making rather than fundamental technical limitations
implicitAssumptions: Alternative lithography approaches (like NIL) are technically viable but rejected due to institutional risk aversion rather than technical merit
The current trajectory of diminishing returns is a choice rather than a physical inevitability
Incumbent chipmakers' organizational structures systematically filter out high-risk, high-reward technical bets

positions.claim: Frivolous litigation against semiconductor companies materially harms U.S. competitiveness in strategic technologies and should be reduced through legal reform
implicitAssumptions: Settlement costs ($100-500M range) represent meaningful opportunity costs that would otherwise fund strategic R&D or capacity expansion
The U.S. legal system's current balance between protecting rights and enabling frivolous suits is suboptimal for national strategic interests
AI tools will asymmetrically empower litigious actors more than defendants, worsening the problem over time

positions.claim: Speed of datacenter deployment creates a durable competitive moat, making onsite power generation strategically valuable despite higher costs
implicitAssumptions: First-mover advantages in AI model training or inference deployment compound over time rather than being easily replicated
The revenue differential from 6-month faster deployment exceeds the lifetime cost premium of onsite generation
Hyperscalers' willingness to abandon grid power signals a permanent shift rather than a temporary capacity crunch
